answer.text: The hedging instrument AND the hedged item are both measured at fair value with gains/losses in P&L
Only the hedging instrument is measured at fair value
Only the hedged item is measured at fair value
Neither is measured at fair value

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Fair value hedge: both the hedging instrument and the hedged item (or portion being hedged) are measured at fair value. Gains/losses on both go to P&L, offsetting each other. **The Trap:** Both sides must be measured at fair value — that's what makes the hedge effective in P&L. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fair value hedge mechanics are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the hedge is effective, gains on one side offset losses on the other, minimizing net P&L impact.
